title: School-based Nursery Capital Grant 2025 to 2026
body: " Grant applications  Applications for the School-based Nursery (SBN) Capital Grant for 2025 to 2026 have now closed.  If you applied, you can sign into the online application service and:   check the status of your application download a copy of your application and any supporting documents   Use the same sign-in details you used to submit your application.  Registration of interest  The registration of interest has also now closed.  If you registered your interest, we may contact you to discuss support options. If we contact your school, it won’t affect the outcome of any future grant application.  Any information you submitted will help develop the programme. We may share it with local authorities.   About the grant  The SBN Capital Grant for 2025 to 2026 was available for eligible state-funded primary-phase schools and maintained nursery schools to apply for up to £150,000 of capital funding to create or expand a school-based nursery.  This is phase 2 of the school-based nurseries programme. It builds on the foundations of phase 1 (the SBN Capital Grant for 2024 to 2025) and has an increased focus on supporting disadvantaged families to access early years provision.  For more information, read the School-based Nursery Capital Grant 2025 to 2026  (PDF, 718 KB, 50 pages) guidance.  What happens next  By April 2026, we aim to email all applicants with the outcome of their application. We will also publish outcomes on GOV.UK.  If your application is successful, we will:   send you a grant funding agreement to sign before we release any funds give you more details about the payment process, reporting requirements and timelines before you sign it   The agreement is legally binding and outlines:   your roles and responsibilities the terms and conditions you must follow to receive the funding   If your application is unsuccessful, you may be eligible to work with your local authority as part of phase 3. More information is available in the guidance.  Contact us  We encourage all applicants to sign up to receive emails about updates to this page. If you have a query about the school-based nurseries programme, use the customer help portal to contact us.  "
